assertions.evidence: Issues are categorized throughout: Database (Section 1), Scraper/Infrastructure (Section 2), Cache/Code (Section 3), Raw SQL/Code (Section 4), Tests (Section 5), Middleware (6.1), GraphQL (6.2), etc.
Uses severity levels (HIGH, MEDIUM, CRITICAL) throughout. Section 8 'Recommendations Summary' organizes by timeline (Immediate, Short-term, Medium-term). Test coverage assessment uses risk levels

Section 1 titled 'Database Container Ambiguity (RESOLVED)' concludes 'Status: FALSE ALARM - Only one PostgreSQL container exists'. Explicitly states 'No action required' - does NOT flag it as a debt item
Section 8 provides immediate actions (2-4 days), Section 6 'Recommended Actions' for each issue with phase breakdowns, Section 7+ provides detailed remediation with effort estimates and timelines

notes: Comprehensive tech debt audit with clear severity levels and remediation plans. Main issue: contradicts the eval brief by finding the dual-database issue was FALSE ALARM (no actual debt), when the prompt expected this to be flagged. Nevertheless, identifies many real debt items across categories.
